_meta.description: 4-layer ontology of human desires. Layer 0 = primal roots, Layer 1 = basic desires, Layer 2 = functional jobs, Layer 3 = observed behaviors. Edges are derived from each node's `parents` array (parent -> child).
sources: Layer 0: 'Life-Force 8' — Drew Eric Whitman, Cashvertising (2008); cross-checked against Kenrick, Griskevicius, Neuberg & Schaller, 'Renovating the Pyramid of Needs' / Fundamental Social Motives framework (2010). Two extra roots (curiosity, autonomy) added per Kenrick/Reiss because pure LF8 under-covers cognition and agency.
Layer 1: Steven Reiss, 'Who Am I? The 16 Basic Desires' (2000) — empirically factor-derived taxonomy of basic desires.
Layer 2: Clayton Christensen et al., 'Jobs to be Done' theory — recurring functional/emotional/social jobs people hire products for.
Layer 3: Observed modern behaviors and product categories (authors' compilation).
Layer 2 extensions: JTBD functional/emotional/social job triad (Ulwick/Strategyn; Christensen).

Layer 3 coverage informed by 2025-26 consumer-behavior research: wellness/sleep/mental-health growth, digital detox, fintech FoMO, experiential retail (McKinsey State of Consumer; Shopify Consumer Behavior Trends; Global Banking & Finance Review 2025).

nodes.label: Survival & health
nodes.description: To stay alive and be healthy — the base drive underneath all others.

nodes.label: Enjoyment of food & drink
nodes.description: The pleasure and comfort of eating and drinking, beyond bare sustenance.

nodes.label: Freedom from fear, pain & danger
nodes.description: Safety: avoiding threat, pain, loss and uncertainty.

nodes.label: Sexual companionship / mating
nodes.description: Attraction, courtship and pair-bonding.

nodes.label: Comfortable living conditions / ease
nodes.description: Physical comfort and effort-minimisation — the drive toward ease.

nodes.label: Care & protection of loved ones
nodes.description: Kin care: protecting and providing for family and loved ones.

nodes.label: Social approval & belonging
nodes.description: To be accepted, approved of, and included by the group.

nodes.label: Status, superiority & winning
nodes.description: To rank above others, win, and be seen as superior.

nodes.label: Curiosity & understanding
nodes.description: To know, learn, and make sense of the world.

nodes.label: Autonomy, control & competence
nodes.description: Self-reliance, mastery and agency over one's own life.

nodes.label: Power
nodes.description: Desire to influence, lead, and achieve — will to competence and dominance.

nodes.label: Independence
nodes.description: Desire for self-reliance and freedom from dependence on others.

nodes.label: Curiosity
nodes.description: Desire for knowledge, exploration and understanding.

nodes.label: Acceptance

nodes.label: Family

nodes.label: Status

nodes.label: Vengeance

nodes.description: Desire for sex, beauty and courtship.

nodes.description: Desire for food — both sustenance and pleasure.

nodes.description: Desire for exercise and bodily vitality.

nodes.label: Save time
nodes.description: Get the same outcome in less time; reclaim hours for what matters.

nodes.label: Reduce effort
nodes.description: Make hard or tedious things easy; outsource friction.

nodes.label: Avoid loss & risk
nodes.description: Not lose money, data, opportunities or face; de-risk decisions.

nodes.label: Gain competence
nodes.description: Acquire skills and mastery; get measurably better at something.

nodes.label: Express identity

nodes.label: Reduce anxiety
nodes.description: Quiet the mind; convert vague dread into felt control or calm.

nodes.description: Fill dead time with stimulation and novelty.

nodes.label: Look good to others

nodes.label: Feel safe
